description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical body composed of vertical wooden staves, bound together by two prominent dark metal hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood has a natural, slightly weathered finish, with visible grain and some areas of discoloration, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood, tightly fitted within the metal bands. One side of the barrel features a set of visible scratches or marks, adding to its worn, utilitarian character. The metal hoops are riveted and appear robust, providing structural reinforcement. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage yards, or near workshops, serving as a container or decorative object.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with dark metal hoops, flat wooden ends, and visible grain and scratches, typical of urban storage or decor.
description_view_0: The barrel is seen with its wooden staves and both metal hoops clearly visible, showing a clean, unmarked surface.
description_view_1: This view highlights the cylindrical shape and the even placement of the metal hoops, with no visible markings.
description_view_2: This side displays a set of distinct scratches or marks on the wood, adding character and a sense of use.
description_view_3: The barrel appears uniform, with the wood grain and metal hoops visible, and no significant markings.
